content.text:
This is the classic Putnam 1987 B1: the integral evaluates to 1 via the substitution $x \to 6-x$, since $f(x) + f(6-x) = 1$.

Let me write the solution.

input.content:
import Mathlib

open Real MeasureTheory intervalIntegral

noncomputable def putnam_1987_b1_solution : ℝ := 1

theorem putnam_1987_b1 :
    ∫ x in (2:ℝ)..4, Real.sqrt (Real.log (9 - x)) /
      (Real.sqrt (Real.log (9 - x)) + Real.sqrt (Real.log (x + 3)))
    = putnam_1987_b1_solution := by
  unfold putnam_1987_b1_solution
  -- Let f(x) = √ln(9-x) / (√ln(9-x) + √ln(x+3))
  -- Key fact: f(x) + f(6-x) = 1 for x ∈ [2,4]
  -- Then 2I = ∫₂⁴ 1 = 2, so I = 1.
  set f : ℝ → ℝ := fun x => Real.sqrt (Real.log (9 - x)) /
      (Real.sqrt (Real.log (9 - x)) + Real.sqrt (Real.log (x + 3))) with hf_def
  -- First show: for x ∈ (2,4), f(x) + f(6-x) = 1
  have hkey : ∀ x ∈ Set.Ioo (2:ℝ) 4, f x + f (6 - x) = 1 := by
    intro x hx
    simp only [hf_def]
    -- 9 - (6 - x) = x + 3, and (6 - x) + 3 = 9 - x
    have h1 : (9 : ℝ) - (6 - x) = x + 3 := by ring
    have h2 : (6 - x) + 3 = 9 - x := by ring
    rw [h1, h2]
    -- Now need √a/(√a+√b) + √b/(√b+√a) = 1
    set a := Real.sqrt (Real.log (9 - x))
    set b := Real.sqrt (Real.log (x + 3))
    -- Need a + b > 0, equivalently a > 0 ∨ b > 0
    have hxlt : x < 4 := hx.2
    have hxgt : 2 < x := hx.1
    have hpos1 : Real.log (9 - x) > 0 := by
      apply Real.log_pos
      linarith
    have hpos2 : Real.log (x + 3) > 0 := by
      apply Real.log_pos
      linarith
    have ha : a > 0 := Real.sqrt_pos.mpr hpos1
    have hb : b > 0 := Real.sqrt_pos.mpr hpos2
    have hab : a + b > 0 := by linarith
    have hba : b + a > 0 := by linarith
    field_simp
    ring
  -- Substitution: ∫₂⁴ f(6-x) dx = ∫₂⁴ f(x) dx
  have hsub : ∫ x in (2:ℝ)..4, f (6 - x) = ∫ x in (2:ℝ)..4, f x := by
    have := intervalIntegral.integral_comp_sub_left f 6 (a := 2) (b := 4)
    -- integral_comp_sub_left : ∫ x in a..b, f (c - x) = ∫ x in (c-b)..(c-a), f x
    simp at this
    convert this using 1
    norm_num
  -- Continuity to allow integration
  have hcont : ContinuousOn f (Set.Icc (2:ℝ) 4) := by
    simp only [hf_def]
    apply ContinuousOn.div
    · apply ContinuousOn.sqrt
      apply ContinuousOn.log
      · fun_prop
      · intro x hx
        have : x ≤ 4 := hx.2
        linarith
    · apply ContinuousOn.add
      · apply ContinuousOn.sqrt
        apply ContinuousOn.log
        · fun_prop
        · intro x hx
          have : x ≤ 4 := hx.2
          linarith
      · apply ContinuousOn.sqrt
        apply ContinuousOn.log
        · fun_prop
        · intro x hx
          have : 2 ≤ x := hx.1
          linarith
    · intro x hx
      have hxlt : x ≤ 4 := hx.2
      have hxgt : 2 ≤ x := hx.1
      have hpos1 : Real.log (9 - x) > 0 := Real.log_pos (by linarith)
      have hpos2 : Real.log (x + 3) > 0 := Real.log_pos (by linarith)
      have ha : Real.sqrt (Real.log (9 - x)) > 0 := Real.sqrt_pos.mpr hpos1
      have hb : Real.sqrt (Real.log (x + 3)) > 0 := Real.sqrt_pos.mpr hpos2
      linarith
  have hint_f : IntervalIntegrable f MeasureTheory.volume 2 4 :=
    hcont.intervalIntegrable_of_Icc (by norm_num)
  -- Continuity of g(x) = f(6-x) on [2,4]
  have hcont_g : ContinuousOn (fun x => f (6 - x)) (Set.Icc (2:ℝ) 4) := by
    apply ContinuousOn.comp hcont (by fun_prop)
    intro x hx
    constructor
    · have : x ≤ 4 := hx.2; linarith
    · have : 2 ≤ x := hx.1; linarith
  have hint_g : IntervalIntegrable (fun x => f (6 - x)) MeasureTheory.volume 2 4 :=
    hcont_g.intervalIntegrable_of_Icc (by norm_num)
  -- Combine: ∫₂⁴ (f(x) + f(6-x)) dx = ∫₂⁴ 1 dx = 2
  have hsum : ∫ x in (2:ℝ)..4, (f x + f (6 - x)) = 2 := by
    have heq : ∀ x ∈ Set.uIoc (2:ℝ) 4, f x + f (6 - x) = 1 := by
      intro x hx
      rw [Set.uIoc_of_le (by norm_num : (2:ℝ) ≤ 4)] at hx
      apply hkey
      exact ⟨hx.1, hx.2⟩
    rw [intervalIntegral.integral_congr_ae]
    · simp
      norm_num
    · rw [Filter.EventuallyEq, MeasureTheory.ae_iff]
      apply MeasureTheory.measure_mono_null _ (MeasureTheory.measure_singleton (2:ℝ))
      sorry
  -- Now: ∫₂⁴ f + ∫₂⁴ f(6-x) = 2, but ∫₂⁴ f(6-x) = ∫₂⁴ f, so 2 ∫₂⁴ f = 2
  have hsum2 : ∫ x in (2:ℝ)..4, f x + ∫ x in (2:ℝ)..4, f (6 - x) = 2 := by
    rw [← intervalIntegral.integral_add hint_f hint_g]
    exact hsum
  rw [hsub] at hsum2
  linarith

content.text: Let me fix the proof to use `integral_congr` properly with the Icc instead of Ioo.

input.content:
import Mathlib

open Real MeasureTheory intervalIntegral

noncomputable def putnam_1987_b1_solution : ℝ := 1

theorem putnam_1987_b1 :
    ∫ x in (2:ℝ)..4, Real.sqrt (Real.log (9 - x)) /
      (Real.sqrt (Real.log (9 - x)) + Real.sqrt (Real.log (x + 3)))
    = putnam_1987_b1_solution := by
  unfold putnam_1987_b1_solution
  set f : ℝ → ℝ := fun x => Real.sqrt (Real.log (9 - x)) /
      (Real.sqrt (Real.log (9 - x)) + Real.sqrt (Real.log (x + 3))) with hf_def
  -- Key fact: f(x) + f(6-x) = 1 for x ∈ [2,4]
  have hkey : ∀ x ∈ Set.Icc (2:ℝ) 4, f x + f (6 - x) = 1 := by
    intro x hx
    simp only [hf_def]
    have h1 : (9 : ℝ) - (6 - x) = x + 3 := by ring
    have h2 : (6 - x) + 3 = 9 - x := by ring
    rw [h1, h2]
    have hxlt : x ≤ 4 := hx.2
    have hxgt : 2 ≤ x := hx.1
    have hpos1 : Real.log (9 - x) > 0 := Real.log_pos (by linarith)
    have hpos2 : Real.log (x + 3) > 0 := Real.log_pos (by linarith)
    have ha : Real.sqrt (Real.log (9 - x)) > 0 := Real.sqrt_pos.mpr hpos1
    have hb : Real.sqrt (Real.log (x + 3)) > 0 := Real.sqrt_pos.mpr hpos2
    have hab : Real.sqrt (Real.log (9 - x)) + Real.sqrt (Real.log (x + 3)) > 0 := by linarith
    have hba : Real.sqrt (Real.log (x + 3)) + Real.sqrt (Real.log (9 - x)) > 0 := by linarith
    field_simp
    ring
  -- Continuity of f on [2,4]
  have hcont : ContinuousOn f (Set.Icc (2:ℝ) 4) := by
    simp only [hf_def]
    apply ContinuousOn.div
    · apply ContinuousOn.sqrt
      apply ContinuousOn.log
      · fun_prop
      · intro x hx
        have : x ≤ 4 := hx.2; linarith
    · apply ContinuousOn.add
      · apply ContinuousOn.sqrt
        apply ContinuousOn.log
        · fun_prop
        · intro x hx
          have : x ≤ 4 := hx.2; linarith
      · apply ContinuousOn.sqrt
        apply ContinuousOn.log
        · fun_prop
        · intro x hx
          have : 2 ≤ x := hx.1; linarith
    · intro x hx
      have hxlt : x ≤ 4 := hx.2
      have hxgt : 2 ≤ x := hx.1
      have hpos1 : Real.log (9 - x) > 0 := Real.log_pos (by linarith)
      have hpos2 : Real.log (x + 3) > 0 := Real.log_pos (by linarith)
      have ha : Real.sqrt (Real.log (9 - x)) > 0 := Real.sqrt_pos.mpr hpos1
      have hb : Real.sqrt (Real.log (x + 3)) > 0 := Real.sqrt_pos.mpr hpos2
      linarith
  have hint_f : IntervalIntegrable f MeasureTheory.volume 2 4 :=
    hcont.intervalIntegrable_of_Icc (by norm_num)
  -- Substitution: ∫₂⁴ f(6-x) dx = ∫₂⁴ f(x) dx
  have hsub : ∫ x in (2:ℝ)..4, f (6 - x) = ∫ x in (2:ℝ)..4, f x := by
    have h := intervalIntegral.integral_comp_sub_left f 6 (a := 2) (b := 4)
    simp only at h
    -- h : ∫ x in 2..4, f (6 - x) = ∫ x in (6-4)..(6-2), f x
    have h6 : (6:ℝ) - 4 = 2 := by norm_num
    have h7 : (6:ℝ) - 2 = 4 := by norm_num
    rw [h6, h7] at h
    exact h
  -- Continuity of g(x) = f(6-x) on [2,4]
  have hcont_g : ContinuousOn (fun x => f (6 - x)) (Set.Icc (2:ℝ) 4) := by
    apply ContinuousOn.comp hcont (by fun_prop)
    intro x hx
    refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
    · have : x ≤ 4 := hx.2; linarith
    · have : 2 ≤ x := hx.1; linarith
  have hint_g : IntervalIntegrable (fun x => f (6 - x)) MeasureTheory.volume 2 4 :=
    hcont_g.intervalIntegrable_of_Icc (by norm_num)
  -- Combine: ∫₂⁴ (f(x) + f(6-x)) dx = ∫₂⁴ 1 dx = 2
  have hsum : ∫ x in (2:ℝ)..4, (f x + f (6 - x)) = 2 := by
    have heq : Set.EqOn (fun x => f x + f (6 - x)) (fun _ => (1:ℝ)) (Set.uIcc (2:ℝ) 4) := by
      intro x hx
      rw [Set.uIcc_of_le (by norm_num : (2:ℝ) ≤ 4)] at hx
      exact hkey x hx
    rw [intervalIntegral.integral_congr heq]
    simp
    norm_num
  -- Now: ∫₂⁴ f + ∫₂⁴ f(6-x) = 2, but ∫₂⁴ f(6-x) = ∫₂⁴ f, so 2 ∫₂⁴ f = 2
  have hsum2 : ∫ x in (2:ℝ)..4, f x + ∫ x in (2:ℝ)..4, f (6 - x) = 2 := by
    rw [← intervalIntegral.integral_add hint_f hint_g]
    exact hsum
  rw [hsub] at hsum2
  linarith

    hcont_g.intervalIntegrable_of_Icc (by norm_num)
  -- Combine: ∫₂⁴ (f(x) + f(6-x)) dx = ∫₂⁴ 1 dx = 2
  have hsum : ∫ x in (2:ℝ)..4, (f x + f (6 - x)) = 2 := by
    have heq : Set.EqOn (fun x => f x + f (6 - x)) (fun _ => (1:ℝ)) (Set.uIcc (2:ℝ) 4) := by
      intro x hx
      rw [Set.uIcc_of_le (by norm_num : (2:ℝ) ≤ 4)] at hx
      exact hkey x hx
    rw [intervalIntegral.integral_congr heq]
    simp
    norm_num
  -- Now: ∫₂⁴ f + ∫₂⁴ f(6-x) = 2, but ∫₂⁴ f(6-x) = ∫₂⁴ f, so 2 ∫₂⁴ f = 2
  have hsum2 : ∫ x in (2:ℝ)..4, f x + ∫ x in (2:ℝ)..4, f (6 - x) = 2 := by
    rw [← intervalIntegral.integral_add hint_f hint_g]
    exact hsum
  rw [hsub] at hsum2
  linarith

tool_use_result.originalFile:
import Mathlib

open Real MeasureTheory intervalIntegral

noncomputable def putnam_1987_b1_solution : ℝ := 1

theorem putnam_1987_b1 :
    ∫ x in (2:ℝ)..4, Real.sqrt (Real.log (9 - x)) /
      (Real.sqrt (Real.log (9 - x)) + Real.sqrt (Real.log (x + 3)))
    = putnam_1987_b1_solution := by
  unfold putnam_1987_b1_solution
  -- Let f(x) = √ln(9-x) / (√ln(9-x) + √ln(x+3))
  -- Key fact: f(x) + f(6-x) = 1 for x ∈ [2,4]
  -- Then 2I = ∫₂⁴ 1 = 2, so I = 1.
  set f : ℝ → ℝ := fun x => Real.sqrt (Real.log (9 - x)) /
      (Real.sqrt (Real.log (9 - x)) + Real.sqrt (Real.log (x + 3))) with hf_def
  -- First show: for x ∈ (2,4), f(x) + f(6-x) = 1
  have hkey : ∀ x ∈ Set.Ioo (2:ℝ) 4, f x + f (6 - x) = 1 := by
    intro x hx
    simp only [hf_def]
    -- 9 - (6 - x) = x + 3, and (6 - x) + 3 = 9 - x
    have h1 : (9 : ℝ) - (6 - x) = x + 3 := by ring
    have h2 : (6 - x) + 3 = 9 - x := by ring
    rw [h1, h2]
    -- Now need √a/(√a+√b) + √b/(√b+√a) = 1
    set a := Real.sqrt (Real.log (9 - x))
    set b := Real.sqrt (Real.log (x + 3))
    -- Need a + b > 0, equivalently a > 0 ∨ b > 0
    have hxlt : x < 4 := hx.2
    have hxgt : 2 < x := hx.1
    have hpos1 : Real.log (9 - x) > 0 := by
      apply Real.log_pos
      linarith
    have hpos2 : Real.log (x + 3) > 0 := by
      apply Real.log_pos
      linarith
    have ha : a > 0 := Real.sqrt_pos.mpr hpos1
    have hb : b > 0 := Real.sqrt_pos.mpr hpos2
    have hab : a + b > 0 := by linarith
    have hba : b + a > 0 := by linarith
    field_simp
    ring
  -- Substitution: ∫₂⁴ f(6-x) dx = ∫₂⁴ f(x) dx
  have hsub : ∫ x in (2:ℝ)..4, f (6 - x) = ∫ x in (2:ℝ)..4, f x := by
    have := intervalIntegral.integral_comp_sub_left f 6 (a := 2) (b := 4)
    -- integral_comp_sub_left : ∫ x in a..b, f (c - x) = ∫ x in (c-b)..(c-a), f x
    simp at this
    convert this using 1
    norm_num
  -- Continuity to allow integration
  have hcont : ContinuousOn f (Set.Icc (2:ℝ) 4) := by
    simp only [hf_def]
    apply ContinuousOn.div
    · apply ContinuousOn.sqrt
      apply ContinuousOn.log
      · fun_prop
      · intro x hx
        have : x ≤ 4 := hx.2
        linarith
    · apply ContinuousOn.add
      · apply ContinuousOn.sqrt
        apply ContinuousOn.log
        · fun_prop
        · intro x hx
          have : x ≤ 4 := hx.2
          linarith
      · apply ContinuousOn.sqrt
        apply ContinuousOn.log
        · fun_prop
        · intro x hx
          have : 2 ≤ x := hx.1
          linarith
    · intro x hx
      have hxlt : x ≤ 4 := hx.2
      have hxgt : 2 ≤ x := hx.1
      have hpos1 : Real.log (9 - x) > 0 := Real.log_pos (by linarith)
      have hpos2 : Real.log (x + 3) > 0 := Real.log_pos (by linarith)
      have ha : Real.sqrt (Real.log (9 - x)) > 0 := Real.sqrt_pos.mpr hpos1
      have hb : Real.sqrt (Real.log (x + 3)) > 0 := Real.sqrt_pos.mpr hpos2
      linarith
  have hint_f : IntervalIntegrable f MeasureTheory.volume 2 4 :=
    hcont.intervalIntegrable_of_Icc (by norm_num)
  -- Continuity of g(x) = f(6-x) on [2,4]
  have hcont_g : ContinuousOn (fun x => f (6 - x)) (Set.Icc (2:ℝ) 4) := by
    apply ContinuousOn.comp hcont (by fun_prop)
    intro x hx
    constructor
    · have : x ≤ 4 := hx.2; linarith
    · have : 2 ≤ x := hx.1; linarith
  have hint_g : IntervalIntegrable (fun x => f (6 - x)) MeasureTheory.volume 2 4 :=
    hcont_g.intervalIntegrable_of_Icc (by norm_num)
  -- Combine: ∫₂⁴ (f(x) + f(6-x)) dx = ∫₂⁴ 1 dx = 2
  have hsum : ∫ x in (2:ℝ)..4, (f x + f (6 - x)) = 2 := by
    have heq : ∀ x ∈ Set.uIoc (2:ℝ) 4, f x + f (6 - x) = 1 := by
      intro x hx
      rw [Set.uIoc_of_le (by norm_num : (2:ℝ) ≤ 4)] at hx
      apply hkey
      exact ⟨hx.1, hx.2⟩
    rw [intervalIntegral.integral_congr_ae]
    · simp
      norm_num
    · rw [Filter.EventuallyEq, MeasureTheory.ae_iff]
      apply MeasureTheory.measure_mono_null _ (MeasureTheory.measure_singleton (2:ℝ))
      sorry
  -- Now: ∫₂⁴ f + ∫₂⁴ f(6-x) = 2, but ∫₂⁴ f(6-x) = ∫₂⁴ f, so 2 ∫₂⁴ f = 2
  have hsum2 : ∫ x in (2:ℝ)..4, f x + ∫ x in (2:ℝ)..4, f (6 - x) = 2 := by
    rw [← intervalIntegral.integral_add hint_f hint_g]
    exact hsum
  rw [hsub] at hsum2
  linarith

import Mathlib

open Real MeasureTheory intervalIntegral

noncomputable def putnam_1987_b1_solution : ℝ := 1

theorem putnam_1987_b1 :
    ∫ x in (2:ℝ)..4, Real.sqrt (Real.log (9 - x)) /
      (Real.sqrt (Real.log (9 - x)) + Real.sqrt (Real.log (x + 3)))
    = putnam_1987_b1_solution := by
  unfold putnam_1987_b1_solution
  set f : ℝ → ℝ := fun x => Real.sqrt (Real.log (9 - x)) /
      (Real.sqrt (Real.log (9 - x)) + Real.sqrt (Real.log (x + 3))) with hf_def
  -- Key fact: f(x) + f(6-x) = 1 for x ∈ [2,4]
  have hkey : ∀ x ∈ Set.Icc (2:ℝ) 4, f x + f (6 - x) = 1 := by
    intro x hx
    simp only [hf_def]
    have h1 : (9 : ℝ) - (6 - x) = x + 3 := by ring
    have h2 : (6 - x) + 3 = 9 - x := by ring
    rw [h1, h2]
    have hxlt : x ≤ 4 := hx.2
    have hxgt : 2 ≤ x := hx.1
    have hpos1 : Real.log (9 - x) > 0 := Real.log_pos (by linarith)
    have hpos2 : Real.log (x + 3) > 0 := Real.log_pos (by linarith)
    have ha : Real.sqrt (Real.log (9 - x)) > 0 := Real.sqrt_pos.mpr hpos1
    have hb : Real.sqrt (Real.log (x + 3)) > 0 := Real.sqrt_pos.mpr hpos2
    have hab : Real.sqrt (Real.log (9 - x)) + Real.sqrt (Real.log (x + 3)) > 0 := by linarith
    have hba : Real.sqrt (Real.log (x + 3)) + Real.sqrt (Real.log (9 - x)) > 0 := by linarith
    field_simp
    ring
  -- Continuity of f on [2,4]
  have hcont : ContinuousOn f (Set.Icc (2:ℝ) 4) := by
    simp only [hf_def]
    apply ContinuousOn.div
    · apply ContinuousOn.sqrt
      apply ContinuousOn.log
      · fun_prop
      · intro x hx
        have : x ≤ 4 := hx.2; linarith
    · apply ContinuousOn.add
      · apply ContinuousOn.sqrt
        apply ContinuousOn.log
        · fun_prop
        · intro x hx
          have : x ≤ 4 := hx.2; linarith
      · apply ContinuousOn.sqrt
        apply ContinuousOn.log
        · fun_prop
        · intro x hx
          have : 2 ≤ x := hx.1; linarith
    · intro x hx
      have hxlt : x ≤ 4 := hx.2
      have hxgt : 2 ≤ x := hx.1
      have hpos1 : Real.log (9 - x) > 0 := Real.log_pos (by linarith)
      have hpos2 : Real.log (x + 3) > 0 := Real.log_pos (by linarith)
      have ha : Real.sqrt (Real.log (9 - x)) > 0 := Real.sqrt_pos.mpr hpos1
      have hb : Real.sqrt (Real.log (x + 3)) > 0 := Real.sqrt_pos.mpr hpos2
      linarith
  have hint_f : IntervalIntegrable f MeasureTheory.volume 2 4 :=
    hcont.intervalIntegrable_of_Icc (by norm_num)
  -- Substitution: ∫₂⁴ f(6-x) dx = ∫₂⁴ f(x) dx
  have hsub : ∫ x in (2:ℝ)..4, f (6 - x) = ∫ x in (2:ℝ)..4, f x := by
    have h := intervalIntegral.integral_comp_sub_left f 6 (a := 2) (b := 4)
    simp only at h
    -- h : ∫ x in 2..4, f (6 - x) = ∫ x in (6-4)..(6-2), f x
    have h6 : (6:ℝ) - 4 = 2 := by norm_num
    have h7 : (6:ℝ) - 2 = 4 := by norm_num
    rw [h6, h7] at h
    exact h
  -- Continuity of g(x) = f(6-x) on [2,4]
  have hcont_g : ContinuousOn (fun x => f (6 - x)) (Set.Icc (2:ℝ) 4) := by
    apply ContinuousOn.comp hcont (by fun_prop)
    intro x hx
    refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
    · have : x ≤ 4 := hx.2; linarith
    · have : 2 ≤ x := hx.1; linarith
  have hint_g : IntervalIntegrable (fun x => f (6 - x)) MeasureTheory.volume 2 4 :=
    hcont_g.intervalIntegrable_of_Icc (by norm_num)
  -- Combine: ∫₂⁴ (f(x) + f(6-x)) dx = ∫₂⁴ 1 dx = 2
  have hsum : ∫ x in (2:ℝ)..4, (f x + f (6 - x)) = 2 := by
    have heq : Set.EqOn (fun x => f x + f (6 - x)) (fun _ => (1:ℝ)) (Set.uIcc (2:ℝ) 4) := by
      intro x hx
      rw [Set.uIcc_of_le (by norm_num : (2:ℝ) ≤ 4)] at hx
      exact hkey x hx
    rw [intervalIntegral.integral_congr heq]
    simp
    norm_num
  -- Now: ∫₂⁴ f + ∫₂⁴ f(6-x) = 2, but ∫₂⁴ f(6-x) = ∫₂⁴ f, so 2 ∫₂⁴ f = 2
  have hsum2 : ∫ x in (2:ℝ)..4, f x + ∫ x in (2:ℝ)..4, f (6 - x) = 2 := by
    rw [← intervalIntegral.integral_add hint_f hint_g]
    exact hsum
  rw [hsub] at hsum2
  linarith

lines: -- Now: ∫₂⁴ f + ∫₂⁴ f(6-x) = 2, but ∫₂⁴ f(6-x) = ∫₂⁴ f, so 2 ∫₂⁴ f = 2
-  have hsum2 : ∫ x in (2:ℝ)..4, f x + ∫ x in (2:ℝ)..4, f (6 - x) = 2 := by
